title: Scaevola kauaiensis
taxonomy: Scaevola kauaiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae, native to the Hawaiian Islands. This species is a member of the genus Scaevola, which comprises approximately 60 species of shrubs and herbaceous plants. The Goodeniaceae family, to which Scaevola kauaiensis belongs, is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the goodenia family. The classification of Scaevola kauaiensis is further detailed at the rank of order, with the plant being classified as a member of the asterids, an order of flowering plants. The classification of Scaevola kauaiensis is as follows: Scaevola kauaiensis, Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Goodeniaceae, Genus: Scaevola, Species: S. kauaiensis.

intro: Scaevola kauaiensis, also known as Hawaiian blue eyes or Ka'uawa, is a perennial plant native to the Hawaiian Islands, belonging to the family Goodeniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Scaevola kauaiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been isolated and identified through various studies. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory infections.
